seoTitle: Alton Downs vs Amamoor — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Alton Downs and Amamoor. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Alton Downs vs Amamoor: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Alton Downs and Amamoor.
heading: Alton Downs vs Amamoor — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Alton Downs, Queensland and Amamoor, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Amamoor has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Alton Downs (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Alton Downs or Amamoor safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Alton Downs has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Amamoor scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Alton Downs scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Amamoor scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Alton Downs or Amamoor?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Amamoor has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
